title: Swedish government's anti-immigrant escalation sparks backlash among population
subhead: With a few months to go before the September 13 elections, several new reforms are set to further toughen the conditions for migrants and refugees in the country. Minors born in Sweden, individuals who have lived there for decades and whose children are Swedish citizens now face deportation.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
AI Actors and Writers Not Eligible for Oscars
The labor market is sidelining older workers in Europe and in the United States
subhead: Older workers face employment challenges due to both discrimination and the economic calculations of recruiters, according to a report from the research and statistics office of the French Ministry of Labor (DARES).

title: Cuba calls US sanctions 'collective punishment' during May 1 marches
subhead: In an executive order on May 1, US President Donald Trump announced sanctions on people involved in wide-sweeping sectors of the Cuban economy, including those who operate in 'energy and 'defense.' Cuba's foreign minister called the new measures 'collective punishment' of the nation's people.

title: The Lebrun brothers and the French team's dream of a first world table tennis title is 'in the back of their minds'
subhead: With five players ranked in the world Top 30, the French national table tennis team is once again a strong contender for a medal at the World Team Championships, the final stage of which is taking place from May 2 to May 10 in London.

title: Wales's pro-independence party hopes to top polls for first time ever
subhead: On May 7, regional and local elections in Wales could see the Labour Party suffer a historic defeat, leaving the pro-independence Plaid Cymru and the far-right Reform UK in a tight race for control of the Welsh government.

Russia drastically scales back May 9 parade over fears of Ukrainian drone attacks
subhead: For the first time since 2008, the victory parade will feature no military hardware. In recent months, Ukraine has repeatedly carried out drone strikes deep inside Russia.

title: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
subhead: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

title: In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhead: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher
